title: 10 best herbal teas for emphysema
intro_desc: Herbal teas for emphysema are infusions made from plants and herbs that can help alleviate the symptoms of this chronic lung condition. They are commonly used as a complementary therapy to improve breathing, reduce inflammation, and soothe the throat. Some examples of herbal teas that can help with emphysema include licorice root, coltsfoot, and mullein. Licorice root tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, while coltsfoot tea can help loosen phlegm and mucus in the lungs. Mullein tea is known for its expectorant properties, which can help clear the airways and improve breathing. By using these herbal teas, individuals with emphysema may experience improved lung function, reduced coughing and wheezing, and a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: New England aster tea helps with emphysema because it contains flavonoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing. Additionally, it contains antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. The tea may also help to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, which can help clear airways and improve oxygen flow. Furthermore, New England aster tea has been traditionally used to treat respiratory conditions, making it a natural choice for those looking to manage emphysema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: New England aster tea can reduce inflammation in the airways, which is important for emphysema as it helps alleviate breathlessness and improves oxygen flow, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: The tea's high antioxidant properties help protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which is crucial for emphysema patients as it can slow the progression of the disease, thanks to compounds such as chlorogenic acid and rutin.

Expectorant: New England aster tea promotes the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, which is beneficial for emphysema as it aids in clearing airways and reducing lung congestion, due to its active constituents like saponins and tannins.

Bronchodilator: The tea can help widen the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe, which is vital for emphysema patients as it alleviates symptoms of breathlessness and improves overall lung function, due to compounds like essential oils and terpenoids.

Immune-boosting: New England aster tea supports the immune system, which is important for emphysema patients as it can help the body fight off infections and reduce the risk of exacerbations, thanks to its active constituents like vitamins C and E, and various minerals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried New England aster flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free of debris and contaminants.
Rinse 16 ounces of filtered water and boil it, then let it cool for a few minutes to reach a temperature between 200-212°F.
Place the harvested herbs in a teapot or infuser and pour the hot water over them, allowing the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, discarding the solid remains.
Serve the New England aster tea while warm, consuming 2-3 cups daily for best results in managing emphysema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the New England Aster plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe for those with emphysema.

Flowers: The flowers of the New England Aster plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the lungs from damage caused by oxidative stress, making it beneficial for emphysema patients.
Roots: The roots of the New England Aster plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe irritated respiratory tracts, providing relief for emphysema patients.

Seeds: The seeds of the New England Aster plant contain essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for those with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tea helps with emphysema because it contains expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These properties can help clear mucus from the lungs, reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, and protect lung tissue from further damage. The soothing effect of mullein tea can also help alleviate coughing and ease breathing, providing relief to those suffering from emphysema. Furthermore, mullein tea has been traditionally used to treat respiratory conditions, making it a popular natural remedy for emphysema. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Great mullein tea helps clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe, due to its mucilaginous and saponin constituents.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of emphysema, thanks to its verbascoside and iridoid glycosides.
Antitussive: Great mullein tea soothes coughs and suppresses the urge to cough, providing relief for people with emphysema, due to its verbascoside and rotenone constituents.
Antioxidant: The tea neutralizes free radicals and protects lung tissue from damage, slowing the progression of emphysema, due to its high flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Vasodilatory: Great mullein tea dilates blood vessels, improving oxygen supply to the lungs and alleviating shortness of breath, due to its rotenone and coumarin constituents.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for a minute.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mullein leaves to a tea infuser.
Pour the hot water over the mullein leaves and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and discard the used mullein leaves.
Drink the mullein tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the great mullein plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for emphysema due to their expectorant and soothing properties, which help to clear mucus from the lungs.

Flowers: The flowers of the great mullein plant are used in teas for emphysema due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the respiratory tract.
Seeds: The seeds of the great mullein plant are used in teas for emphysema due to their mucilaginous properties, which can help to soothe and protect the respiratory tract.
Stems: The stems of the great mullein plant are used in teas for emphysema due to their demulcent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the respiratory tract.
Roots: The roots of the great mullein plant are used in teas for emphysema due to their expectorant and diuretic properties, which can help to clear mucus from the lungs and promote urination.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with emphysema because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a slick gel when mixed with water. This gel can cover and soothe the throat and respiratory membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation. Additionally, marshmallow tea has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory and soothing effects of marshmallow tea can alleviate some of the symptoms associated with emphysema, such as coughing and shortness of breath. However, it is important to note that while marshmallow tea may provide some relief, it is not a cure for emphysema and should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea is rich in mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, important for emphysema as it helps reduce coughing and ease breathing.

Expectorant: The tea stimulates the production of mucus and aids in its expulsion from the lungs, alleviating congestion and making breathing easier for emphysema patients. This is attributed to the high mucilage content.

Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for emphysema patients. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in the tea are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.
Antitussive: The tea's soothing effect on the respiratory tract can help alleviate persistent coughing, a common symptom of emphysema. This is due to the tea's high mucilage content.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow tea contains antioxidants, which can help protect the lungs from damage caused by oxidative stress, a factor contributing to emphysema. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the tea's flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of mixed dried herbs (such as coltsfoot, licorice root, and thyme), then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea daily, preferably between meals.
Store any remaining tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps soothe irritated membranes in the respiratory tract, making it useful for emphysema.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage and are used to make medicinal teas that help alleviate coughing and other respiratory symptoms associated with emphysema.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing inflammation in the lungs of emphysema patients.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant contain high levels of mucilage, making them a key ingredient in teas that help soothe and moisturize the respiratory tract in emphysema patients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with emphysema because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe, and can also help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs. Additionally, licorice tea may help soothe irritation and inflammation in the throat and chest, providing relief from symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. However, it is important to note that people with certain medical conditions, such as high blood pressure, kidney disease, or liver disease, should avoid consuming large amounts of licorice tea, as it can have adverse effects. As always, it is best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating emphysema symptoms.
Expectorant: The expectorant effect of licorice tea, attributed to its saponin content, helps clear mucus from the respiratory tract, benefiting those with emphysema.
Antispasmodic: Licorice tea's antispasmodic properties, resulting from the action of glycyrrhizin, can help relax the bronchial smooth muscles, easing breathing for emphysema patients.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of licorice tea, due to the existence of flavonoids, may help protect against respiratory infections, which can further complicate emphysema.

Demulcent: Licorice tea's demulcent effect, caused by its mucilage content, forms a protective coating on the mucous membranes, providing soothing relief for irritated respiratory tracts in emphysema sufferers.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of this herbal tea daily, as needed, to help manage emphysema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe for those with emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the licorice plant have expectorant properties that help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief for emphysema symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by emphysema.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain essential oils that have soothing properties, which can help to alleviate coughing and wheezing associated with emphysema.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain coumarins that have blood thinning properties, which can help to improve circulation and oxygenation of the blood for those with emphysema.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise tea helps with emphysema because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, anise tea has been shown to have expectorant properties, which can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, alleviating congestion and improving overall lung function. Furthermore, anise tea can help soothe coughs and reduce chest pain, providing relief to those suffering from emphysema. However, it is important to note that while anise tea may provide some symptomatic relief, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or prescribed medications for emphysema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Anise tea can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, helping to alleviate symptoms of emphysema by potentially decreasing swelling and irritation in the airways. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like anethole, estragole, and flavonoids.

Bronchodilator: Anise tea can help dilate the bronchioles, making it easier to breathe by improving airflow to the lungs. This is attributed to the presence of anethole, the primary active compound in anise, which has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscles.

Expectorant: Anise tea can help loosen mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel them from the respiratory tract. This property is due to the presence of anethole, which has been found to have expectorant properties and can help clear congestion.

Antimicrobial: Anise tea has antimicrobial properties that can help protect against respiratory infections, which are common in people with emphysema. This is due to the presence of active constituents like trans-anethole, estragole, and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial properties.

Antioxidant: Anise tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of emphysema. This property is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties and can help reduce oxidative stress in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried anise seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the anise seeds.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the anise plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe for those with emphysema.
Leaves: The leaves of the anise plant have expectorant properties, which can help loosen mucus and phlegm in the airways, making it easier to expel and clear the airways for those with emphysema.
Seeds: The seeds of the anise plant contain compounds that can help relax the muscles of the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe for those with emphysema.
Flowers: The flowers of the anise plant have antimicrobial properties, which can help protect the respiratory tract from infections, which can be particularly dangerous for those with emphysema.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the anise plant contain antioxidants that can help protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate emphysema symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Chop one tablespoon of dried fennel seeds and place them in a tea infuser.
Boil eight ounces of water and pour it over the fennel seeds in the infuser.
Let the tea steep for five to ten minutes.
Remove the infuser and discard the used fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with emphysema because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme tea can help to thin and expel mucus from the lungs, which can reduce congestion and improve overall lung function. The herb's volatile oils, such as thymol, have been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect the lungs from damage caused by infection and inflammation. Furthermore, thyme tea has been used traditionally to treat respiratory ailments, including bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, and its soothing and calming effects can help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress associated with emphysema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating emphysema symptoms, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and volatile oils.
Expectorant: Its expectorant effects aid in thinning and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe, thanks to the active constituents, such as thymol and carvacrol.
Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties help protect the respiratory system from infections, which can exacerbate emphysema, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of thyme tea help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage, slowing the progression of emphysema, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Bronchodilator: Thyme tea can help relax and widen the airways, improving breathing and oxygen uptake, through the action of active components like thymol and eucalyptol.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Place the thyme leaves in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the thyme leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea before drinking. Enjoy up to 3 cups of thyme tea daily for emphysema relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which help to reduce inflammation and relax airways, making it easier to breathe.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and antiseptic compounds, which can help to clear congestion and soothe irritated lungs in emphysema.
Stems: Thyme stems contain rosmarinic acid and other beneficial compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
Seeds: Thyme seeds are a rich source of antioxidants and have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions like emphysema.
Rhyzomes: Thyme rhyzomes contain high levels of essential oils and have been used to treat respiratory infections and inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with emphysema because it contains rosmarinic acid, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties. This compound can help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, marjoram tea is a natural expectorant, which can help clear mucus from the lungs and improve overall respiratory function. The tea's antioxidant properties can also help protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals. Furthermore, marjoram tea has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscles, which can help alleviate symptoms of emphysema such as coughing and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Marjoram tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, common issues in emphysema.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's active compounds, including terpinene, alpha-terpineol, and sabinene hydrate, have been shown to reduce inflammation, thus alleviating breathing difficulties and lung damage in emphysema.

Bronchodilator properties: Marjoram tea has bronchodilator effects due to its volatile oils, which help widen the airways, making it easier for individuals with emphysema to breathe and reducing shortness of breath.

Expectorant properties: The tea's expectorant properties, attributed to its essential oils and tannins, help loosen mucus and phlegm in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and alleviating congestion and coughing in emphysema.

Calming properties: Marjoram tea contains calming compounds, like linalool and linalyl acetate, that can help soothe the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress associated with emphysema and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried marjoram leaves, letting it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Steep - After boiling, remove water from heat and add marjoram leaves, allowing it to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Strain the tea to remove the marjoram leaves, discarding them afterwards.
Sweeten (optional) - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve - Enjoy the herbal marjoram tea while warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for emphysema due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with emphysema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, rosemary contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to help dilate the bronchioles, improving airflow to the lungs. Furthermore, rosemary tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking rosemary tea can also help soothe a persistent cough and reduce phlegm, providing relief to those suffering from emphysema. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before adding any new herbal remedies to a treatment plan.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating emphysema symptoms, due to the presence of active compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in rosemary tea helps protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage, which is crucial for emphysema management, thanks to compounds such as carnosol and carnosic acid.

Bronchodilator: Rosemary tea can help dilate the airways, improving breathing and oxygen flow, by stimulating the production of cAMP (cyclic adenosine monophosphate), which is facilitated by the presence of 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Antispasmodic: Its antispasmodic properties help relax smooth muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing and shortness of breath, due to active constituents like rosmarinic acid and verbascoside.

Mucolytic: Rosemary tea can help thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs, as a result of its mucolytic effect, which is attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the rosemary leaves.
Drink the tea slowly, up to three times a day for best results.

Flowers: High in flavonoids, rosemary flowers can help improve respiratory function and reduce inflammation in the airways, making them useful for emphysema tea.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat emphysema with herbal teas is by using a blend of licorice, coltsfoot, and thyme. Licorice soothes the throat and reduces inflammation, while coltsfoot helps to loosen phlegm and expand the airways. Thyme has antiseptic properties that can help fight off infections. Drinking these teas can help alleviate symptoms, but it's important to remember that herbal teas are not a cure for emphysema. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to emphysema that are treated with herbal teas include chronic bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. Herbal teas such as licorice root, coltsfoot, and marshmallow root are often used to alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. These herbs work by soothing inflammation in the respiratory tract, thinning mucus, and supporting overall lung health. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy, especially for individuals with pre-existing lung conditions.
